assertions.title: Fresh baselines render as normal range bands on frontend
assertions.reason: Night view heart-rate strip rendered a dedicated baseline reference band element (`.baseline-band`) with `fill-opacity` 0.12 (<=15%) spanning the full plot width; API baseline values were present for the same date.

evidence.network: GET /api/views/night?date=2026-03-29 -> 200 (captured via curl artifact because agent-browser request capture was empty)

assertions.title: S3 scenario post-workout recovery arc visible at a glance
assertions.reason: Recovery arc and narrative are visible (Readiness 42->84, HRV 26->50, insight text includes 'over 3 days'), but the UI did not show an explicit visible '10K run' marker label in rendered page text; only generic 'workout' markers were visible, so the full assertion contract was not met.

evidence.network: GET /api/views/recovery?start=2026-03-22&end=2026-03-26 -> 200 (captured via curl artifact because agent-browser request capture was empty)

frictions.description: `agent-browser click` on Heart Rate strip header was blocked by an overlay despite visible element ref.
frictions.resolution: Used in-page click via `agent-browser eval` to expand the strip and capture baseline-band evidence.

frictions.description: `agent-browser network requests` returned no captured entries for dashboard API calls.
frictions.resolution: Captured equivalent local request/response evidence with authenticated curl and stored headers/body artifacts.

summary: Retested 2 assertions: 1 passed (VAL-CROSS-007), 1 failed (VAL-UIRCV-003). Baseline band rendering now validates with explicit low-opacity full-width band evidence; recovery arc narrative is present but explicit visible '10K run' label evidence remains missing on the rendered UI.
